step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture shows a patient record access and document storage pipeline. Clinicians access the platform via NHS N3/HSCN network through Direct Connect. This routes to the microservices-based Integration Service (EC2) with its own RDS database. The Integration Service coordinates with the FHIR Service (EC2 with RDS) to normalize structured data. Unstructured letters/documents are uploaded via Storage Gateway into S3, which triggers SQS to invoke Lambda. Lambda hits the Document Service (EC2 with RDS) to index the metadata in PostgreSQL RDS and store document content back to S3.

graph.name: Kainos Multi-Tenant Healthcare Interoperability Platform
graph.notes: Microservices platform handling FHIR structured patient data and unstructured documents for 650,000 patients with 2,000 concurrent active care workers.

nodes.name: Clinicians / Care Workers
nodes.notes: Access the platform through secure N3/HSCN NHS network.

nodes.name: AWS Direct Connect
nodes.notes: Secures data flows into AWS environment from the NHS network.

nodes.name: Integration Service
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Microservice handling initial requests and third-party data retrieval.

nodes.name: FHIR Service
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Standardizes healthcare data into the FHIR format.

nodes.name: FHIR RDS
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Postgres RDS storing standardized FHIR records.

nodes.name: AWS Storage Gateway
nodes.notes: Bridges local healthcare systems to mirror unstructured documents.

nodes.name: S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores raw and processed clinical documents.

nodes.name: Lambda Function
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Processes queue messages to trigger metadata ingestion.

nodes.name: Document Service
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Microservice managing document metadata and content locations.

edges.notes: Clinicians log in and request patient records.
Mirrors local documents (discharge letters) into S3.
Triggers processing event on object creation.
Calls Document Service to process ingested file.
Saves processed clinical document to production S3 bucket.
